purpose: This commit fixes a potential use-after-free bug in Bluetooth HCI command queuing. It ensures hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error when HCI_UNREGISTER is set, preventing commands from being queued after device unregistration.

The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c. It clarifies that linux/tty.h already pulls in linux/termios.h, making the explicit include redundant and potentially confusing.

This commit updates documentation for the 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c. It reflects changes in the report table after adding the 'N/A' metric for store operations, ensuring documentation aligns with code changes.

This commit fixes a race condition in hugetlb vma lock handling during vma splitting and range unmapping. It addresses issues where vma lock pointers were incorrectly copied during splitting, causing potential double-free errors, and ensures proper lock management for all VM_MAYSHARE vmas.

This commit adds device tree binding documentation for AVDD and MICVDD supplies used by the rt5682s codec. It ensures these power supplies are properly documented in the device tree bindings, aligning with existing usage in sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi.

This commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver. It adds a missing iounmap() call in the remove function to properly release memory mapping resources that were allocated during probe but not freed during removal.
Removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox 5 network driver. The change is a cleanup of dead code, improving code clarity and reducing memory footprint.
The commit changes the type of 'key_idx' in 'cfg80211_auth_request' from unsigned to signed. This prevents static analyzer warnings when -1 is assigned to indicate unused keys.

This commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in RISC-V defconfigs. The change aligns the order with savedefconfig's output, ensuring consistency. No functional changes are expected.
The commit introduces a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip is in CAN-FD mode, replacing an inline check. It's a code refactoring improving maintainability.
The commit modifies RTC selftests to skip execution when no RTC device is present, preventing test failures in such environments.

The commit removes a redundant variable assignment in the ALSA echoaudio driver. The variable 'bytes' is assigned but never read, then reassigned in a subsequent if block. This cleanup eliminates a clang scan build warning.

This commit allows the rcuscale feature to be built without requiring RCU Tasks. It decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing operations in CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y kernels.
The commit removes a duplicated 'be split' string in the spte.h file, correcting a typo that could affect code clarity and maintainability.

This commit reverts a change that populated a DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The revert prevents a no-read situation caused by how the PMFW fills the table when DCFCLK states are disabled, avoiding potential underflow.
This commit adds device tree binding documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU, including a new compatible string to enable GPU support in the kernel.

The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with naming conventions in the DW PCIe driver. It updates all references to the struct to maintain functionality while improving code clarity.
Fixes a Clang compiler warning by correcting the format specifier for an unsigned short argument in a netdev_dbg call. Changes %hx to %x to match promoted argument type.
The commit fixes a branch range check issue in MIPS jump_label implementation by casting the upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing a warning triggered by negative offsets.

This commit removes a specific case statement HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD from the SetHwReg8188EU() function. It simplifies the function by calling the underlying function directly, eliminating unnecessary code. This is part of a larger effort to reduce the HAL layer.

The commit declares non-global symbols as static in arm64 architecture code to fix sparse warnings. It addresses a specific issue where a symbol was not declared as static, causing a warning during kernel compilation.

This commit corrects incorrect interrupt flag specifications in MDSS display driver bindings. It removes bogus interrupt flags cells from consumer nodes, as the MDSS interrupt provider uses a single cell.
The commit allocates space for arrays in the struct ctrl_ref to prepare for allowing drivers to change array sizes. It modifies memory allocation for control references in V4L2 subsystem.

Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) to the qcom socinfo driver. This expands hardware compatibility by including new device identifiers in the SoC information table.
The commit updates the RTL8xxxu WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (letters A to P) instead of only up to E, preventing 'unknown' output for newer chip versions.
This commit removes the custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks in the ux500 clock driver. It leverages the core's existing software enable count, simplifying the clock management logic.

The commit refactors RISC-V memory management code to reduce reliance on CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL preprocessor directives. It groups related #ifdef usage and moves macro definitions closer to their variables for better code organization.

This commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for the sm6350-lena board, preparing the hardware for future I2C node additions. It modifies the device tree to expose DMA capabilities.
This commit fixes comments in the rzg2l thermal driver, correcting a misleading description, fixing a typo, and aligning with coding style by placing /* on a separate line.

Fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver's release_swfw_sync function. The commit handles the case where acquiring the HW semaphore fails, typically due to hardware surprise removal, by logging an error and exiting gracefully.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing older bus clock defines. It is a code style and terminology improvement without functional changes.
The commit removes the IS_SWAPFILE hack because the macro is always defined, making the code redundant. It's a straightforward cleanup change.

This commit implements GTP echo response functionality in the kernel. It handles GTP-U packets with echo requests by sending appropriate echo responses, including recovery tags and restart counters as per GTP specification.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* functions where appropriate, improving performance. It also introduces a new cpumask_first_zero() function to handle cases where no such function existed.

This commit removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. The removal prevents an error message from appearing during STA roaming when firmware no longer supports this functionality.

Adds support for a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the sof_rt5682 driver, enabling audio codec alc5682 without speaker amplifier, connected via SSP0. Follows Intel BT offload design patterns.

This commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail gracefully when the underlying socket protocol does not support zerocopy. It extends a previous fix for send requests to sendmsg operations, preventing potential errors or undefined behavior.

The commit modifies the kerneldoc preamble to conditionally load xeCJK.sty only if it exists, preventing xelatex errors when the package is missing. This fixes a specific issue on RHEL 9 and clones where texlive-xecjk is unavailable.

This commit modifies checkpatch.pl to allow 'case' macros without requiring parentheses around complex values. It addresses a false positive error in the script when processing certain kernel driver files.
The commit converts drm/simpledrm from simple-KMS helpers to regular atomic helpers. It improves code architecture for easier driver code sharing, with no functional changes.
This commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel execution of three hardware units on MT8195. It introduces new code to manage hardware concurrency for JPEG decoding.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from libata, as their information is already covered by tracepoints. It also removes ata_scsi_dump_cdb() since scsi tracing can provide the same data.
This commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD display drivers. It enables entering zstate when transitioning to FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO, using extra vblank time for optimization.
Fixes a CFI violation by aligning the reset_type parameter type in hqd_destroy() callback from enum kfd_preempt_type to uint32_t, matching the actual function pointer prototype.
Corrects the property name 'reset' to 'resets' in pinctrl device tree bindings. Adds 'additionalProperties: false' to prevent future errors.

The commit silences an array-bounds warning in the ath9k wireless driver. It addresses a GCC 12 compiler warning by reclassifying the issue as a W=1+ warning, not fixing a functional bug but preventing a compile-time warning.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate runtime power management function.

The commit reorganizes io_uring file installation and removal logic by moving it into the filetable code section. It also changes helper functions to accept a ctx instead of an io_kiocb, as the operation is related to the ring rather than individual requests.

This commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder nodes in the device tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU. It reflects that these are independent blocks sharing only a power domain, and updates the bindings to support separate nodes with power-domain controller support.
The commit removes a macro IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON that is only used once, inlining its contents. This is a minor refactoring to simplify the codebase.
